documents.content: Dynamic programming framework that works for 80% of DP problems: (1) Identify the state — what are the parameters that uniquely define a subproblem? Usually 1-2 dimensions: index in array, capacity remaining, position in two strings, etc. (2) Write the recurrence — given the answer to smaller subproblems, how do you compute the current answer? This is usually 1-3 cases based on choices at the current step. (3) Identify base cases — smallest subproblems with trivial answers. (4) Choose top-down (memoization with recursion) or bottom-up (iterative with table). Top-down is easier to write; bottom-up is faster and more memory-efficient. (5) Optimize space if the recurrence only depends on the last 1-2 rows/columns.

Classic DP patterns to master: (1) 1D DP on arrays — climb stairs, house robber, longest increasing subsequence. State is index in array. (2) Knapsack family — 0/1 knapsack, coin change, subset sum, partition equal subset sum. State is (index, capacity). (3) String DP — longest common subsequence, edit distance, regex matching. State is (i, j) where i, j are positions in the two strings. (4) Interval DP — burst balloons, matrix chain multiplication. State is (left, right) defining a range. (5) Tree DP — house robber III, binary tree max path sum. Use postorder traversal and return tuples of states. (6) Bitmask DP — traveling salesman variants. State includes a bitmask of visited nodes.

How to spot a DP problem: keywords like 'maximum', 'minimum', 'longest', 'shortest', 'count the number of ways', 'is it possible'. The problem involves making choices at each step, and earlier choices constrain later ones. Brute force gives exponential time and you notice repeated subproblems. If you can write a recursion and it has overlapping subproblems, DP works. If the recursion has no overlap (each subproblem is unique), it's not DP — likely just plain recursion or backtracking. Common beginner mistake: trying DP when greedy or two pointers would work; classic example is 'best time to buy and sell stock' which is just O(n) one-pass tracking min so far.
